explanation: The requirement states that whenever both prop_1 and prop_2 are true, prop_0 should also be true. In LTL, this is expressed using the 'globally' operator to say that at all times, if prop_1 and prop_2 hold, then prop_0 must hold.
This requirement specifies that 'prop_0' must hold whenever both 'prop_1' and 'prop_2' hold. This is classically represented as globally, if prop_1 and prop_2, then prop_0.

The requirement states that whenever both prop_1 and prop_2 are true, the autopilot must do prop_0. In LTL, this means that it is always the case (G) that if prop_1 and prop_2 are true, then prop_0 is true as well.

This requirement specifies that whenever both prop_1 and prop_2 are true, prop_0 must also be true at that moment. In LTL, we can specify this as a safety property using the 'globally' operator: at all times, if prop_1 and prop_2 hold, then prop_0 must hold.

The sentence structure suggests that prop_0 should hold whenever both prop_1 and prop_2 are true at a given time. That is, at any point in time, if prop_1 and prop_2 are true, then prop_0 must also be true.

The sentence means that 'prop_0' happens at the same time as both 'prop_1' and 'prop_2'. The LTL formula expresses that everywhere globally, if 'prop_1' and 'prop_2' are true, then 'prop_0' must also be true at the same time.

The requirement states that whenever both prop_1 and prop_2 are true, the autopilot shall ensure prop_0. In LTL, this is typically expressed as: globally, if prop_1 and prop_2 hold, then prop_0 must hold as well.

The requirement expresses that whenever both prop_1 and prop_2 are true, prop_0 should also be true. In LTL, this means that globally, at every state, if prop_1 and prop_2 hold, then prop_0 must also hold at that state.

The statement suggests that prop_0 occurs simultaneously with both prop_1 and prop_2. A direct way to express 'when' in LTL is to use implication: whenever prop_1 and prop_2 hold, prop_0 holds as well. This is expressed as globally (G) if (prop_1 and prop_2) hold, then prop_0 must also hold at the same time.
This statement means that whenever both prop_1 and prop_2 are true, then prop_0 should occur at the same time. Globally, if both prop_1 and prop_2 hold, then prop_0 must hold as well.
